Ribbon-like Subcortical Heterotopia and Brain Overgrowth

edges.source: Ribbon-like Subcortical Heterotopia and Brain Overgrowth

nodes.description: Centrosome/spindle perturbation causes apical radial glia to lose their apical anchoring and delaminate from the ventricular zone, so that dividing progenitors become abnormally distributed throughout the cortical wall rather than confined to the ventricular and subventricular zones. Misplaced apical progenitors are the critical initiating event for heterotopia formation, and rescuing microtubule formation in vivo reduces both delamination and heterotopia volume, establishing the causal link.
nodes.id: Apical Radial Glia Delamination and Ectopic Progenitors

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in Eml1 lead to ectopic progenitors and neuronal heterotopia in mouse and human.
evidence.snippet: While HeCo neurons migrated at the same speed as wild type, abnormally distributed dividing progenitors were found throughout the cortical wall from embryonic day 13

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the Heterotopia Gene Eml1/EML1 Severely Disrupt the Formation of Primary Cilia.
evidence.snippet: In HeCo mice, some aRGs are abnormally positioned away from the ventricular zone (VZ)

evidence.reference_title: Forebrain Eml1 depletion reveals early centrosomal dysfunction causing subcortical heterotopia.
evidence.snippet: By rescuing microtubule formation in Eml1 mutant embryonic brains, abnormal radial glia delamination and heterotopia volume were significantly reduced

evidence.reference_title: Human cerebral organoids reveal progenitor pathology in EML1-linked cortical malformation.
evidence.snippet: EML1-deficient organoids display ectopic neural rosettes at the basal side of the ventricular zone areas and clusters of heterotopic neurons.

nodes.description: Microtubule dysregulation in EML1-deficient apical radial glia disrupts the centrosome and primary cilium and abnormally elongates the mitotic spindle. Cilia are shorter, less numerous and aberrantly oriented within vesicles, and centrosomes and microtubule regrowth are impaired in both mouse mutant radial glia and human patient cells, placing the proximal lesion at the progenitor centrosome/spindle apparatus.
nodes.id: Centrosome, Cilium and Mitotic Spindle Perturbation in Apical Radial Glia

evidence.reference_title: Eml1 loss impairs apical progenitor spindle length and soma shape in the developing cerebral cortex.
evidence.snippet: centrosome and primary cilia were altered and spindles were found to be abnormally long in HeCo progenitors

evidence.snippet: In HeCo aRGs, cilia are shorter, less numerous, and often found aberrantly oriented within vesicles

nodes.description: Biallelic loss-of-function variants in EML1 abolish full-length EMAP-1, a microtubule-associated protein expressed in cortical apical radial glia. In the spontaneous HeCo mouse, a retrotransposon insertion eliminates full-length Eml1 transcripts, and knockdown reproduces the progenitor phenotype while re-expression rescues it, establishing EML1 loss as the initiating molecular lesion. EML1 loss perturbs microtubule dynamics in progenitor cells, the proximal cytoskeletal defect from which the centrosome, cilium and spindle abnormalities follow.
nodes.id: EML1 Loss of Function and Microtubule Dysregulation

nodes.description: Ectopic progenitors retained within the cortical wall continue to divide and generate neurons outside their normal niche. These neurons are produced and trapped ectopically, accounting for the excessive number of cortical neurons found in the subcortical white matter rather than in the cortical plate.
nodes.id: Ectopic Neurogenesis and Cortical Neuron Misplacement

evidence.snippet: Subcortical heterotopia is a cortical malformation associated with epilepsy, intellectual disability, and an excessive number of cortical neurons in the white matter

nodes.description: The distorted progenitor output produces the characteristic malformation: bilateral megalencephaly with a giant ribbon-like subcortical heterotopia, partial or complete callosal agenesis and an overlying polymicrogyria-like cortex. EML1 mutations underlie ribbon-like heterotopia in humans, and the spindle, ectopic-progenitor and heterotopia phenotypes are linked across mouse and human.
nodes.id: Ribbon-like Subcortical Heterotopia and Brain Overgrowth
